title: analyze-ats-score MCP Tool Contract
description: Analyzes resume content for ATS compatibility and returns a score with recommendations

tool.name: analyze-ats-score
tool.description: Analyzes resume content for ATS (Applicant Tracking System) compatibility. Returns a numerical score (0-100), identified issues, and actionable recommendations for improvement.

jobDescription.description: Optional job description for targeted analysis

detailLevel.description: Level of detail in analysis (default: detailed)

location.description: Section or line where issue occurs (optional)

impact.description: Points deducted due to this issue

input.content:
# Jane Doe

**Email**: jane@example.com | **Phone**: +12345678901 | **Location**: San Francisco, CA

## Professional Summary

Senior Software Engineer with 8 years of experience in full-stack development.

## Work Experience

### Senior Software Engineer at TechCorp
**San Francisco, CA** | **01/2020 - Present**

- Architected microservices handling 1M+ daily requests
- Led team of 6 engineers in agile development
- Reduced deployment time by 60% through CI/CD optimization

### Software Engineer at StartupCo
**Remote** | **06/2017 - 12/2019**

- Developed React-based dashboard serving 10K+ users
- Implemented RESTful APIs using Node.js and Express

## Education

### BS Computer Science - MIT
**Cambridge, MA** | **Graduated: 05/2016**
GPA: 3.8

## Skills

**Languages**: JavaScript, Python, TypeScript, Java
**Frameworks**: React, Node.js, Django, Spring Boot
**Tools**: Docker, Kubernetes, AWS, Jenkins

issues.description: Consider adding LinkedIn profile URL for better visibility
Resume could benefit from more quantifiable achievements

recommendations.title: Add LinkedIn Profile
recommendations.description: Include your LinkedIn profile URL in the contact section. This is a standard expectation for tech roles and helps ATS match your profile.

recommendations.title: Quantify More Achievements
recommendations.description: Add specific metrics to remaining accomplishments (e.g., 'Improved performance by X%', 'Managed team of Y people'). You have some good examples but more would strengthen the resume.

strengths: Clear section structure with standard headings
Strong keyword presence for technical roles

input.content:
# Bob Smith

bob.smith@email.com | 555-1234

## About Me

I am a passionate developer who loves coding!

## Experience

Worked at Company A from 2020 to now
- Did some programming
- Helped the team

Worked at Company B in 2018-2019
- Made websites

## School

Graduated from University with a degree

## What I Know

JavaScript, HTML, CSS and some other stuff

issues.description: Phone number not in E.164 format (+1234567890)
Non-standard section heading 'About Me' (should be 'Professional Summary')
Non-standard section heading 'What I Know' (should be 'Skills')
Work experience lacks proper job title format (should be ### Job Title at Company)
Dates not in standard format (should be MM/YYYY - MM/YYYY or Present)
Vague responsibilities lacking specific achievements or metrics
Weak keyword presence - missing specific technologies and frameworks
Education section lacks detail (degree type, institution name, graduation date)
Personal pronouns used ('I am', 'I') - should use objective language

recommendations.title: Use Standard Section Headings
recommendations.description: Replace non-standard headings with ATS-recognized ones: 'Professional Summary' instead of 'About Me', 'Education' instead of 'School', 'Skills' instead of 'What I Know'. This ensures ATS can properly parse your resume sections.

recommendations.title: Format Work Experience Properly
recommendations.description: Use proper heading structure: '### [Job Title] at [Company Name]' followed by location and dates in bold. Include specific dates in MM/YYYY format.

recommendations.title: Add Quantifiable Achievements
recommendations.description: Replace vague statements like 'Did some programming' with specific accomplishments: 'Developed 5 production features serving 10K+ users' or 'Reduced load time by 40%'.

recommendations.title: Expand Technical Skills
recommendations.description: List specific technologies, frameworks, tools, and methodologies. Instead of 'some other stuff', name concrete skills: specific frameworks (React, Vue), tools (Git, Docker), methodologies (Agile, TDD).

recommendations.title: Fix Phone Number Format
recommendations.description: Convert phone to E.164 format: +1234567890. This ensures ATS can properly extract and validate your contact information.

recommendations.title: Detail Education Background
recommendations.description: Include full degree name, major, institution name, location, and graduation date. Format: '### [Degree] - [Major] - [Institution]'

recommendations.title: Remove Personal Pronouns
recommendations.description: Remove 'I', 'me', 'my' from all sections. Use objective language: 'Passionate developer' instead of 'I am a passionate developer'.

testScenarios.description: Should return high score for well-formatted ATS-friendly resume

input.content:
# John Doe

**Email**: john@example.com | **Phone**: +12345678901 | **Location**: New York, NY
**LinkedIn**: https://linkedin.com/in/johndoe

## Professional Summary

Senior Engineer with 10 years of experience in enterprise software development.

## Work Experience

### Lead Engineer at TechCompany
**New York, NY** | **01/2020 - Present**

- Architected scalable microservices handling 5M+ requests/day
- Led team of 8 engineers delivering 20+ features quarterly
- Reduced infrastructure costs by 35% through optimization

### Senior Developer at SoftwareCo
**Boston, MA** | **06/2015 - 12/2019**

- Developed customer-facing platform serving 100K+ users
- Implemented CI/CD pipeline reducing deployment time by 70%

## Education

### MS Computer Science - Stanford University
**Stanford, CA** | **Graduated: 05/2015**
GPA: 3.9

### BS Computer Engineering - MIT
**Cambridge, MA** | **Graduated: 05/2013**

## Skills

**Languages**: Java, Python, JavaScript, TypeScript, Go
**Frameworks**: Spring Boot, Django, React, Node.js, Express
**Tools**: Docker, Kubernetes, Jenkins, AWS, Terraform
**Methodologies**: Agile, Scrum, TDD, CI/CD

assertions: output.score should be >= 85
output.rating should be 'excellent'
output.breakdown.formatting should be >= 25
output.breakdown.keywords should be >= 20
output.breakdown.technical should be >= 20
output.breakdown.readability should be >= 15
output.issues.length should be <= 3
output.strengths.length should be >= 8
breakdown scores should sum to total score

testScenarios.description: Should identify critical issues in poorly formatted resume

input.content:
Bob
email: bob@email.com
phone: 1234567890

I worked at companies and did things.

I went to school.

I know programming.

assertions: output.score should be <= 40
output.rating should be 'poor'
output.issues should have at least 5 critical severity issues
output.issues should include MISSING_REQUIRED_SECTION or similar
output.recommendations.length should be >= 5
recommendations should be sorted by potentialGain descending

assertions: output.issues should include issue with category='structure' for missing Education
output.issues should include issue with category='structure' for missing Skills
output.recommendations should include recommendation about adding Education section
output.recommendations should include recommendation about adding Skills section
